turns.en: No, it's super fun. Again, if you're not ready for this, totally fine. If you're just not going to be in the terminal, totally fine. Play with Claude Code if that feels a little bit more accessible to you. Play with ChatGPT. Play with Claude. Play with any of these tools that feel accessible to you, but start to think: if I could employ someone in my life that I can't actually afford, if I could employ an assistant that just would make my life better, what are the things that they would do? And can AI, in whatever format, get you there? That's a lot of the inspiration and what we do at the podcast is we just try to help you open your eyes to like this can help solve a problem for you, whether that problem is highly technical or not at all technical. I just want people to continue exploring because it's pretty incredible what these tools can do.
turns.zh: 不，这非常有趣。再说一次，如果你还没准备好，完全没问题。如果你就是不想用终端，完全没问题。如果 Claude Code 对你来说更容易上手，那就用它。试试 ChatGPT，试试 Claude，试试任何你觉得容易使用的工具，但开始思考：如果我能雇佣一个我实际上雇不起的人，如果我能雇佣一个能让我的生活变得更好的助手，他们会做什么？而 AI，无论以何种形式，能否帮你实现？这就是很多灵感的来源，我们在播客中所做的就是试图帮你睁开眼睛，看到这可以帮你解决一个问题，无论那个问题是高度技术性的还是完全非技术性的。我只是希望人们继续探索，因为这些工具能做的事情非常不可思议。

turns.en: With that, Claire, we have reached our very exciting lightning round. I've got five questions for you. Are you ready?
turns.zh: 那么，Claire，我们到了非常激动人心的快问快答环节。我有五个问题要问你。准备好了吗？

turns.en: Yeah, I was thinking about this. I'm going to talk about a category of books that I've started to recommend to people, which is classic children's books. If you are a parent out there, you know there's so much children's book slop out there. Graphic novels with like seven words per page, the writing's atrocious. Even in the older years, it doesn't get better.
turns.zh: 是的，我考虑过这个问题。我要说的是我开始推荐给人们的一类书，那就是经典儿童读物。如果你是一位家长，你知道市面上有很多粗制滥造的儿童书。每页只有七个字的图画小说，写作质量糟糕。即使是大一点孩子的书，也好不到哪里去。

turns.en: And we've just started pulling off the shelf like Treasure Island. We are reading Alice in Wonderland. We're reading some of the Shakespearean comedies. The language is so much more sophisticated and my kids still get it and they still think it's funny. And so I feel like we have dumbed down what we expect of our children, especially in what they consume from a media perspective. And so I'm just going to the classics and we're having a really good time. So I highly recommend pulling some of those classic children's books off the shelf, even if they feel a little bit more advanced. My kids have been loving them.
turns.zh: 我们刚开始从书架上拿下来像《金银岛》这样的书。我们在读《爱丽丝梦游仙境》，还在读一些莎士比亚的喜剧。这些书的语言要复杂得多，但我的孩子们仍然能理解，而且觉得很有趣。所以我觉得我们降低了对孩子的期望，尤其是在他们消费的媒体内容方面。所以我直接转向经典作品，我们玩得很开心。我强烈推荐把那些经典儿童读物从书架上拿下来，即使它们感觉有点难。我的孩子们一直很喜欢。

turns.en: I love this tip. Speaking of children's books, my wife's got our children's book coming out in...
turns.zh: 我喜欢这个建议。说到儿童读物，我妻子的童书即将出版……

turns.zh: 婴儿图表。

turns.zh: 婴儿。不知道你有没有看过我们那期节目，我们聊了一点，但很快就要出版了。

I have not watched a movie or a decent television show in nine years. If somebody wants garbage television though, this is the show that I'm talking to your sister about. turns.zh: 哦，这太糟糕了。我其实和你姐姐在一个群聊里，不知道你知不知道，是关于那些糟糕的真人约会节目。我有孩子，已经九年没看过电影或像样的电视剧了。但如果有人想看垃圾电视，这就是我和你姐姐聊的那个节目。《吸引力时代》在 Netflix 上线了，挺糟糕的，但我会全部看完。我在想有没有看过什么真正好的，但没有。我就是从早上 6 点工作到晚上 9 点，然后大脑完全关机。我们不看任何有挑战性的东西。我 21 分钟内就睡着了，连一集都看不完。

turns.zh: 完全关机了。我们不看任何有挑战性的东西。我 21 分钟内就睡着了，连一集都看不完。

turns.zh: 你用什么来追踪睡眠？

turns.zh: 我们用 Aura。

turns.zh: Oura。太棒了。

turns.zh: 是的，Aura。我说我给我的 Aura 充电，这样它就能确认我没在睡觉。对每个人来说，我没睡觉都很明显。我们有全套硅谷睡眠设备。所以，我们有 Aura、Apple Watch，还有 Eight Sleep，它们都说：“Claire，你睡眠不足。”

turns.en: That whole It's a whole bunch of people and you just don't know how old they are. And then they fall in love in Whistler. And then it's like, "He's 70 and she's 19. I wonder if it will work out." And guess what? It is not going to work out.

It's out of battery right now, but I use it to record the podcast, and we also use it to record the kids sports games. And there's this product called Hoop Analytics, where you can upload a video of your kids sports game, and they will annotate the shots, the percentage the passing percentages, how many assists you have, and then my 9-year-old, because we stay on brand here in the Vo Lawless household, my 9-year-old takes that data into Claude and makes a gamma presentation for his coach on what they should focus on for that practice. And so, I think my full AI basketball stack is my favorite kind of product in the family right now. I think it's pretty cool.
turns.zh: 你知道吗？它让我们失望了。但是，这个小云台三脚架，其实不是很贵，我们用它来录播客——我现在用它录播客，但没电了——我们也用它录孩子们的运动比赛。还有一个叫 Hoop Analytics 的产品，你可以上传孩子比赛视频，它会标注投篮、传球成功率、助攻次数。然后我 9 岁的孩子，因为我们 Vo Lawless 家保持品牌调性，他把这些数据放进 Claude，给教练做一个 Gamma 演示，说明训练重点。所以，我觉得我完整的 AI 篮球套件是目前家里最喜欢的产品。我觉得挺酷的。

turns.zh: 就像……我们得有我 9 岁孩子投篮的高质量视频。所以它是一个稳定的……就像一个小手持稳定器。很好用。

turns.zh: 好的，我会分享是什么。上面有个 M 标志。我甚至不知道它叫什么。

turns.en: Yeah, we'll link to it.

turns.zh: 好的，我们会找到的。但很好用，除非你用它录播客时它没电了，然后相机掉在地上。

turns.en: Incredible. I love that whole stack and is he getting better? Is this working this flow?
turns.zh: 难以置信。我喜欢整个套件。他进步了吗？这个流程有效吗？

turns.en: He is getting better. Actually, I'll add one more favorite product to the list. This is actually at the top of the list, which is our They're these things called silent basketballs and silent soccer balls. So, as I said, I am living this city mom life. And so, we don't have a very big backyard, but I have kids that want to bounce a basketball all the time in the house, 24 hours a day. And they're basically like foam basketballs. They're the same size as a basketball, but they're foam, and so you can bounce them inside the house and they don't make any noise. And so, they make them for soccer, and they make them for basketball, and we have so many of them. And then, because I have three boys and this is my life, we have like painter's tape on one of our taller ceilings, and my kid just like shoots basketballs inside the house. But what an innovation, right? What a niche problem to solve, which is sports-oriented kids who don't have enough play space to play outside with parents who don't want to hear It's a silent basketball.
turns.zh: 他确实在进步。实际上，我再加一个最喜欢的产品到列表里。这其实是列表第一，就是所谓的静音篮球和静音足球。就像我说的，我过着城市妈妈的生活。我们没有很大的后院，但我的孩子们整天都想在家里拍篮球。它们基本上是泡沫篮球，和普通篮球一样大，但泡沫做的，所以可以在家里拍，没有噪音。他们也有足球款，我们有很多。然后，因为我有三个男孩，这就是我的生活，我们在一个较高的天花板上贴了油漆工胶带，我的孩子就在家里投篮。但这是多大的创新啊，对吧？解决了一个多么小众的问题：有运动倾向的孩子没有足够的户外活动空间，而父母又不想听到噪音。这就是静音篮球。

turns.en: And these feel like real basketballs, right? They like assume they're close enough to be...
turns.zh: 这些摸起来像真篮球，对吧？它们应该足够接近……

turns.en: They make some that are just as heavy as a basketball. So, they have a very But, they're the same you know, they're the same size. You know, you can dribble. They're like kind of fun to play with. So, I This again, these niche products that solve little things in my life. That's As a product person, those are the ones that I think How do they do market discovery here? Because they found customer of me, and I'm very happy.
turns.zh: 他们有些款和篮球一样重。所以，它们有……但你知道，它们大小一样。你可以运球。玩起来挺有意思的。所以，这些解决我生活中小问题的利基产品。作为产品人，我觉得他们是怎么做市场发现的？因为他们找到了我这个客户，我很开心。

turns.en: Okay, two more questions. Do you have a favorite life motto that you often come back to in work or in life?
turns.zh: 好的，还有两个问题。你有没有最喜欢的人生格言，在工作或生活中经常用到？

turns.en: I've said this before, maybe, but fast beats right is the one that I go to on work. You can probably see I optimize for efficiency. I like to go fast. And so, I often think like first out of the gate is a real advantage. And I think if you were to parse Lenny's data, which I know just went live, and you were to connect to the MCP and query what does Clearbanc care about, I care about speed. And so, I think this moment in particular with AI is such a fun one for me as a builder because my speed ambition is met by the capacity of the tools, and it feels very fun.
turns.zh: 我可能以前说过，但“快胜过正确”是我在工作中遵循的格言。你可能看得出来我追求效率。我喜欢快。所以我经常认为率先出发是真正的优势。而且我觉得，如果你要解析 Lenny 的数据——我知道刚上线——然后连接到 MCP，查询 Clearbanc 关心什么，我关心的是速度。所以，我认为特别是现在这个 AI 时代，作为构建者，我觉得非常有趣，因为我的速度野心被工具的能力满足了，感觉非常棒。

turns.en: And then the one personally that I say a lot, which is, most people you work with won't be at your funeral. And I say this from a real place of experience. I have had colleagues who have had family members pass away or they pass away and so few of the people that they worked with on a day-to-day basis, really sad, but so few of them are at the funeral. And so, when I think about how much mental energy I'm going to give to somebody at work or how much I'm going to allow somebody to keep me up at night or how much I'm going to worry about did I ship this code or get that PR out or respond to that email? I think like literally none of the people in this doc will be at my funeral. Who will? My children. And so I use that as a clarifying lens by which I decide what is what really matters, not what I take seriously or not who I show up with respect to. But like when it's starting to impact me, I think that's a very effective lens to kind of have everything fall away and really focus on what's important. And like my vibe coding tools will probably not be at my funeral, so I will not give them sleepless nights.
turns.zh: 还有我经常说的一句个人感悟：你工作里认识的大多数人，不会来参加你的葬礼。我是从亲身经历说这话的。我有同事的家人去世，或者他们自己去世，结果他们日常共事的人里，来参加葬礼的少得可怜——真的很悲伤，但确实就那么几个人。所以，当我考虑要把多少心力花在工作上某个人身上，或者让谁让我彻夜难眠，或者担心代码有没有部署、PR 有没有合进去、邮件有没有回的时候，我就想：这个文档里的人，没有一个会来我的葬礼。谁会来？我的孩子。所以我用这个作为澄清的透镜，来决定什么才是真正重要的——不是我看重什么，或者我对谁保持尊重。但当它开始影响我的时候，我觉得这是一个非常有效的透镜，能让一切杂念都消失，真正专注于重要的事情。就像我的 vibe coding 工具，大概也不会出现在我的葬礼上，所以我不会为它们失眠。

turns.en: Might be by the time robotics gets there and just... Yeah, poly might be but no one else. Maybe Sage. There's a line along those lines I've shared a couple times in this podcast. Once you hear you'll never forget. It's very heartbreaking. The only people that will remember that you stayed late at work are your kids.
turns.zh: 等到机器人时代到来的时候，可能吧……对，Poly 可能会来，但其他人不会。也许 Sage 会。我在这个播客里分享过几次类似的话。你一旦听到就永远不会忘记，非常令人心碎。唯一会记得你加班到很晚的人，是你的孩子。

turns.en: I mean 100%. 100%. I mean, part of why I like Open Floor, real talk is I'm not on my laptop all the time. And I really kind of hate at this moment. One of the things that is a downside is my kids see me all the time on my laptop. And in some ways I've heard this advice, you know, when you're ever feeling guilty about that, write down all the reasons why you working hard is good for your kids. Like keep a list by your computer of like it's good for my kids to see what hard work looks like. I can provide for my kids. You know, all those things. Keep that list down to remind yourself of that. But I hate that my kids see me hunched over an 18-inch MacBook Air all the time. I don't want that. And so being able to get kind of be unleashed from just your laptop and being able to pop in when they're on the swing to a quick work thing and take care of it and then put it away, or have the peace of mind honestly that my agent team is working on things so I don't have to think about it. It really lets me come closer to my kids versus kind of being chained to the terminal on my laptop.
turns.zh: 我百分之百同意。百分之百。我之所以喜欢 Open Floor 的坦诚对话，部分原因是我并不是一直盯着笔记本电脑。我真的很讨厌现在这样。一个不好的地方是，我的孩子总看到我在用笔记本电脑。某种程度上，我听过这样的建议：当你为此感到内疚时，写下所有你努力工作对孩子有好处的理由。比如在电脑旁放一张清单，写上“让孩子看到努力工作的样子是好事”、“我能供养孩子”等等。把清单放在那里提醒自己。但我讨厌孩子们总看到我弓着背对着 18 英寸的 MacBook Air。我不想要那样。所以，能够从笔记本电脑中解放出来，在孩子们荡秋千的时候快速处理一下工作，然后放下它，或者安心地知道我的智能体团队在帮我处理事情，这样我就不用操心了。这真的让我更接近孩子，而不是被拴在笔记本电脑的终端上。

turns.en: Like we had Mark Andreessen on the podcast and his kid's like coding in Repl.it. He's like 9 years old. He's just like, you know, going hard and I'm guessing he's going to be very successful. So, I'm proud of that. Okay, final question. You're a fancy podcast host now. You started a podcast about a year ago. What's something that surprised you about becoming a podcast host, starting a podcast, doing a podcast?
turns.zh: 就像我们请过马克·安德森上播客，他儿子才 9 岁，就在 Repl.it 上写代码了。他非常投入，我猜他将来会非常成功。所以我为此感到骄傲。好了，最后一个问题。你现在是个很棒的播客主持人了。你大约一年前开始做播客。成为播客主持人、开始做播客、运营播客，有什么让你感到惊讶的吗？

turns.en: I don't know if people give you enough credit. Man, this work is hard. There's a lot I mean that people think, "Oh, you know, you show up and you turn on your laptop and you chit-chat with somebody fun for an hour and you move on." There is so much that goes into the business of podcasting that I have found so interesting to learn because my entire background has been, you know, very similar to yours, in software and startups and technology, which have different revenue models and different business models and different types of work you have to do. And learning the business of podcasting has been really I was surprised at how interesting I have found that. And then, you know, it's a lot of work to get something that looks so effortless on the other side. And so, it's not just the prep and the research and the thumbnails and all that. It's, you know, making sure that you find great guests that you can make look good. It's finding the right timing. And so, I've been really surprised at the complexity of the business. And, you know what? I was not surprised at how good you are at it, but I'm glad we're partners because you're very good at it.
turns.zh: 我不知道人们是否给了你足够的认可。天哪，这工作真难。很多人觉得，“哦，你只要出现，打开笔记本电脑，跟某个有趣的人闲聊一小时，然后继续下一件事就行了。”但播客这个生意背后有太多东西了，我觉得学起来特别有意思，因为我整个背景跟你很像，都是软件、创业和技术领域，那些领域有不同的收入模式、商业模式和需要做的工作。而学习播客的商业模式，我惊讶地发现自己觉得它非常有趣。而且，要让最终成果看起来毫不费力，背后需要付出很多努力。所以不仅仅是准备、研究、做缩略图这些。还包括确保找到优秀的嘉宾，并让他们表现出色；找到合适的时机。所以我对这个行业的复杂性感到非常惊讶。你知道吗？我对你在这方面有多擅长并不惊讶，但我很高兴我们是搭档，因为你真的很厉害。

turns.en: Me too. Claire, you're also amazing at this. I feel like you found your calling in addition to building Chart Your D.
turns.zh: 我也是。克莱尔，你在这方面也很棒。我觉得除了打造 Chart Your D 之外，你找到了自己的使命。

turns.en: Thanks.
turns.zh: 谢谢。

turns.en: Okay, two final questions. Where can find you online if they want to check out all the stuff you're up to, and how can listeners be useful to you?
turns.zh: 好了，最后两个问题。如果听众想了解你正在做的所有事情，在哪里可以找到你？以及听众怎样才能帮到你？

turns.en: Yep, so you can find me on X at Clairevo, similar on LinkedIn. Check out my product chatprd.ai, and of course smash that like and subscribe button on youtube.com/@howiaipod, or Spotify, Apple, wherever you like to get your podcast. I would love for you to follow along. We tell such amazing, fun stories about how people are using AI. We have a lot of laughs, and occasionally Paulie will show up uninvited to the podcast.
turns.zh: 好的，你可以在 X 上找到我，账号是 Clairevo，领英上也是。来看看我的产品 chatprd.ai，当然还有在 youtube.com/@howiaipod 上猛戳点赞和订阅按钮，或者在 Spotify、Apple 等任何你喜欢的播客平台上订阅。我很希望你能关注我们。我们讲述关于人们如何使用 AI 的精彩有趣故事，经常笑个不停，偶尔 Paulie 会不请自来地出现在播客里。

turns.en: That was a good one. Claire, thank you so much for being here.
turns.zh: 说得真好。克莱尔，非常感谢你来做客。

turns.en: Lenny, this was so fun. Thanks.
turns.zh: Lenny，这太有趣了。谢谢。

turns.en: Bye, everyone. Thank you so much for listening. If you found this valuable, you can subscribe to the show on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, or your favorite podcast app. Also, please consider giving us a rating or leaving a review, as that really helps other listeners find the podcast. You can find all past episodes or learn more about the show at lennyspodcast.com. See you in the next episode.
turns.zh: 大家再见。非常感谢收听。如果你觉得本期内容有价值，可以在 Apple Podcasts、Spotify 或你最喜欢的播客应用上订阅本节目。也请考虑给我们评分或写评论，这真的能帮助其他听众找到这个播客。你可以在 lennyspodcast.com 找到所有往期节目或了解更多信息。下期再见。

consensus.en: OpenClaw is a powerful agent framework that can automate personal and professional tasks, saving significant time.
consensus.zh: OpenClaw 是一个强大的智能体框架，可以自动化个人和专业任务，节省大量时间。

consensus.en: Start with one agent, then create multiple specialized agents to avoid context overload and improve performance.
consensus.zh: 从一个智能体开始，然后创建多个专用智能体以避免上下文过载并提高性能。

consensus.en: Treat OpenClaw agents like employees: give them clear roles, access, and progressive trust.
consensus.zh: 将 OpenClaw 智能体视为员工：赋予清晰的角色、权限和渐进式信任。

consensus.en: Use a separate machine or virtual environment for OpenClaw to ensure security and prevent accidental damage.
consensus.zh: 使用单独的机器或虚拟环境运行 OpenClaw，以确保安全并防止意外损坏。

consensus.en: The soul and identity files are key to making agents proactive and aligned with user needs.
consensus.zh: 灵魂和身份文件是让智能体主动且符合用户需求的关键。

consensus.en: Voice notes and ramble mode are high-bandwidth ways to onboard and communicate with agents.
consensus.zh: 语音笔记和漫谈模式是上手和与智能体沟通的高带宽方式。

contrarian.en: OpenClaw's bugs and sharp edges are signs of product-market fit, not failure.
contrarian.zh: OpenClaw 的漏洞和粗糙之处是产品市场契合的标志，而非失败。

contrarian.en: The web is hostile to agents; APIs are often better than browser use for automation.
contrarian.zh: 网络对智能体不友好；API 通常比浏览器使用更适合自动化。

contrarian.en: Being polite to AI agents improves outcomes, just as with human employees.
contrarian.zh: 对 AI 智能体礼貌能改善结果，就像对待人类员工一样。

contrarian.en: Managerial skills (role scoping, onboarding) are more important than technical skills for agent success.
contrarian.zh: 管理技能（角色界定、入职）比技术技能对智能体成功更重要。

contrarian.en: OpenClaw's open-source nature makes it more decomposable and educational than closed-source alternatives.
contrarian.zh: OpenClaw 的开源性质使其比闭源替代品更可分解且更具教育意义。

contrarian.en: Agents can project-manage human tasks (e.g., via Linear tickets), reversing the typical human-AI dynamic.
contrarian.zh: 智能体可以项目管理人类任务（例如通过 Linear 工单），逆转典型的人机动态。
